Per the spec: "quote includes an item with an unknown type (e.g. {type: \"broomstick\"}) → the CLI exits with a non-zero status code and writes an error description to stderr; no `results` are written to stdout". Make the test verify that processScenario throws when given an unknown item type. Use expect(() => processScenario(...)).toThrow().

The compilation succeeded as predicted. Now we have a runtime failure: the test expects a throw, but `processScenario` does NOT throw — it silently processes the unknown type. No empty function is needed since it already exists.
